patches.summary: Set --bq flag default to empty string in promoted_free.go and promoted_suggest.go (was a JSON-encoded Solr literal that the Solr parser rejected).
patches.reason: The generator emits array-typed Solr query params with their JSON-array default value baked into the flag default. When a user runs `free --q foo` without overriding --bq, the JSON array string `["type:provincie^1.5",...]` ships to Solr verbatim, which Solr can't parse and returns HTTP 400. Empty default lets the server-side default apply.
patches.validated_outcome: `pdok-location-pp-cli free --q amsterdam --rows 1` returns 200 with the expected match.

patches.summary: Added Aliases: []string{"geocode"} to the spec-derived `free` command so users can also invoke it as `geocode`.
patches.reason: The spec's operationId yields `free` but the agent-natural and research-aligned name is `geocode`. Adding an alias keeps the spec-derived name while exposing the natural name. README quickstart and SKILL trigger phrases all use `geocode`.
patches.validated_outcome: `pdok-location-pp-cli geocode --q ... --rows 1 --json` returns the same response as `free`.

patches.summary: Patched generic `search` live fallback to hit /bzk/locatieserver/search/v3_1/free instead of /kadaster/location-api/v1/search.
patches.reason: Multi-spec generation picked the Kadaster /search endpoint as the live-fallback URL for the generic search command, but that endpoint requires bracketed per-collection opt-in syntax (`?<col>[version]=1`) that the generic generated path can't construct. Switching the fallback to Locatieserver /free makes search work without forcing users into the OGC opt-in shape; users wanting OGC search use `features search` directly.

patches.summary: Removed `newPdokLocationSearchPromotedCmd` from rootCmd.AddCommand; the function remains so dead-code checks pass but the command is no longer in the Cobra tree.
patches.reason: The raw `/search` endpoint requires `<col>[version]=1` per-collection opt-in that the generic generated path can't encode. Users get the friendly `features search` wrapper instead. The unused AddCommand stays commented in root.go to document why it was removed.
patches.validated_outcome: `pdok-location-pp-cli --help` no longer lists `pdok-location-search`; `features search` works correctly.

patches.summary: Extended doctor.go to add a `sources` block in the report with explicit per-upstream probes for Locatieserver (`/free?q=...`) and Kadaster Location API (`/conformance`), each with status, base path, role, and impact-on-failure.
patches.reason: The base-URL probe (`GET /`) doesn't distinguish which of the two PDOK services is up. When one upstream is degraded the user needs to know which commands still work. The new `doctor --json` output exposes `sources.locatieserver` and `sources.kadaster_location_api` keys so agents can reason about partial availability.
patches.validated_outcome: `pdok-location-pp-cli doctor --json` includes per-source status, role, and impact strings.

patches.summary: Workflow archive's RunE redirects os.Stdout to /dev/null while syncResource runs (in --json mode), then restores it before emitting the final summary JSON.
patches.reason: syncResource emits NDJSON event lines directly to os.Stdout, which interleaves with workflow archive's final JSON summary object — `workflow archive --json` then produced invalid JSON. The redirect suppresses the events under --json while keeping them visible in the human path.
patches.validated_outcome: `pdok-location-pp-cli workflow archive --json | jq .` parses cleanly; non-JSON mode still streams events to stderr.

patches.summary: MCP server entry now supports both stdio (default) and streamable HTTP (MCP_TRANSPORT=http, MCP_HTTP_ADDR=:8080).
patches.reason: Cloud-hosted agents (Claude Desktop's remote install, Managed Agents, web clients) need HTTP transport to connect; stdio-only restricts the server to local installs. The dual-transport binary uses the same tool surface either way.
patches.validated_outcome: `MCP_TRANSPORT=http MCP_HTTP_ADDR=:18080 pdok-location-pp-mcp` listens on /mcp and responds to JSON-RPC; stdio remains the default.

patches.summary: Removed 14 redundant AddTool blocks from internal/mcp/tools.go: 13 `collections_get-<collection>` mirrors and the `pdok-location-search_search` raw endpoint mirror.
patches.reason: The 13 per-collection mirrors are constant-id forms of the parametric `collections_get` tool (one tool that takes the collection id covers them all). `pdok-location-search_search` is the auto-renamed broken endpoint that was already hidden from the CLI. Removing them improves token efficiency for agents loading the MCP tool list.
patches.validated_outcome: Tool count down from 24 to 10 in the hand-written set (the cobratree mirror still surfaces all user-facing Cobra commands).

patches.summary: Replaced long Dutch spec descriptions on the 4 Locatieserver MCP tools (free_free, lookup_lookup, reverse_geocoder, suggest_suggest) and the OGC `pdok-location-api_get-open` tool with concise English summaries.
patches.reason: The spec's Dutch descriptions are 2-3 sentences long and only marginally agent-readable. Concise English summaries reduce MCP token cost per tool list call and read better in English-language tool selectors.

patches.summary: Added 11 hand-coded novel commands per the Phase 1.5 absorb manifest: batch geocode, resolve, nearest, convert (rd-to-ll / ll-to-rd / wkt-to-geojson / geojson-to-wkt), gemeente (get / list / of-point), provincie list, search (FTS over cache; the generic search command shares this), features search, top, perceel lookup.
patches.reason: These were the user-approved transcendence features from Phase 1.5. None of the existing community wrappers (foarsitter/locatieserver, Amsterdam/pdok-api-client, uRosConf/nlgeocoder, oldgeogap/angular-locatieserver-geocoder) ships a batch geocode loop, suggest+lookup chain, multi-source nearest reverse geocode, RD↔WGS84 math, WKT↔GeoJSON conversion, offline gemeente gazetteer, kadastrale-aanduiding parser, or bbox-multi-collection CSV dump. Each command is ~50–150 LoC plus root.go wiring; collectively ~1100 LoC across 9 new files.
patches.validated_outcome: All 11 commands verified live: convert math round-trips to <0.5m precision; resolve returns canonical record with WKT→GeoJSON conversion; nearest returns address+parcel+hectometerpaal+weg+gemeente in one call; gemeente get reads from cached gazetteer (342 gemeenten); batch geocode processes a CSV with cached lookups. Phase 5 full dogfood 128/128 mandatory tests passing.

patches.summary: Added 'Why this CLI bundles two PDOK services' section to SKILL.md explaining the Locatieserver+Location-API combination, with a per-source comparison table. Added Netherlands-only anti-trigger and 'Do not use for mutating remote state' bullets to 'When Not to Use'.
patches.reason: User explicitly asked for the meta description to explain why we chose to combine the two APIs. The anti-trigger explicitly tells agents not to use this CLI for non-Dutch addresses.

patches.summary: Fixed 3 P1 and 4 P2 findings from Greptile code review: partial-coordinate bug (|| vs &&) in nearest and gemeente of-point; removed dead --db flag and false offline-first claim in gemeente of-point; batch geocode no longer populates coord columns on low-score rows; batch geocode checks csv.Writer error after flush; bbox validation now parses each token as float.
patches.reason: Greptile P1: haveLL/haveRD used || so passing only --lat or only --rd-x silently used 0.0 for the missing coordinate, firing reverse calls against a point in the Atlantic. P1: --db flag in gemeente of-point was declared and bound but never read, and the offline-first path described in the Long doc was never implemented. P2: low-score rows in batch geocode fell through to populate coordinate columns despite the error column being set. P2: csv.Writer.Flush() error was silently dropped. P2: bbox only checked for 4 tokens, not valid floats.
patches.validated_outcome: go build ./... passes; partial-pair inputs now return a clear error; gemeente of-point --db flag removed; low-score batch rows no longer emit coordinates; bbox 'a,b,c,d' returns a clear parse error.

patches.summary: Updated perceel lookup Example: field and research.json from the fake 'AMR03 N 1234' aanduiding to the real 'ASD02 A 4332' (an Amsterdam parcel verified via the API).
patches.reason: The original example used a placeholder kadastrale aanduiding that produced a 'not found' error during dogfood probes. The replacement is a real Amsterdam parcel that returns a non-zero match.
